fingerprint: θ = −lonE is the defining identity of the azimuthal equidistant projection. The dome's V13 'discovery' that its angular coordinate equals geographic longitude (negated) is the dome proving it IS the AE projection of the globe — not an independent flat-disc coordinate system.
globe_value: On an AE projection centered on the North Pole, the angular coordinate θ equals geographic longitude λ by definition. The sign convention (East-positive on globe, West-positive on dome) gives θ_dome = −lonE. This identity holds for ANY AE projection of a sphere, regardless of radial mapping.
dome_value: WIN-058 claims θ = −lonE as a 'unified angular coordinate rule' that is a 'confirmed prediction.' V12 used a solar-noon-UTC proxy (θ = (noon_UTC − 12) × 15°) that introduced systematic errors; V13 replaces the proxy with direct longitude.
match_precision: exact (by construction — the dome's angular coordinate IS the globe's longitude)
why_it_matters: This is the most structurally revealing globe fingerprint in the dome model. The dome's coordinate system has two axes: radial (r, derived from WGS84 latitude via H(r)) and angular (θ). WIN-058 establishes that the angular axis is identically the globe's longitude. Combined with the radial axis (which requires WGS84 latitude as input — see WIN-057), this means BOTH dome coordinate axes are globe coordinates in disguise. The dome has no native coordinate system; it is a re-parametrization of WGS84.

analysis.v12_proxy_error: V12's solar noon proxy θ = (noon_UTC − 12) × 15° is approximately equal to −lonE for most cities, but includes equation-of-time contamination. Verification against dome's own city table shows noon-proxy theta matches dome listings to <0.1°, while −lonE diverges by up to 14° (Oslo: −lonE = −10.75° but dome_theta = +3.5° from noon proxy). This means V12 was using solar noon values that differ from longitude — the V13 'fix' replaces the noisy proxy with the clean globe coordinate.
analysis.ae_projection_identity: On any azimuthal equidistant projection centered on the North Pole, the angular coordinate is defined as geographic longitude. This is not a property of flat discs — it is a property of projecting a sphere onto a plane while preserving the angular relationships around the pole. The dome 'discovering' this identity is the dome discovering it is an AE projection.
analysis.angular_vs_radial_asymmetry: The dome's angular coordinate maps perfectly (θ = −lonE, exact), while its radial coordinate requires an exponential correction H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) and still achieves only 5-7% accuracy. This asymmetry is the signature of an AE projection: angles are preserved by construction, but radial distances are distorted because equal latitude increments on a sphere do not correspond to equal radial increments on a flat disc. A native flat-disc coordinate system would have no reason for this angular-perfect/radial-imperfect asymmetry.
analysis.eq_0_9941_context: The 0.9941 EW scale factor from V12 (which coexists with WIN-058's θ = −lonE in the codebase) compensates for the non-linear radial distortion introduced by H(r). On the dome disc, EW arc length at radius r is r × Δθ, which systematically overestimates globe EW distances because the H(r) formula places cities at larger radii than pure AE mapping. The overestimation ratio ranges from 1.05 at lat 55° to 1.31 at lat 25° (computed from H(r)-derived radii). The 0.9941 factor provides a 0.6% contraction — far too small to compensate for this 5-30% systematic error. This confirms 0.9941 was fitted to a specific city pair set, not derived from dome geometry. The dome's own AI context page lists 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as an open question.
analysis.v12_v13_coexistence: WIN-058's detail_evidence notes that distance_analysis.py still applies delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941, meaning the V12 angular scaling and V13's θ = −lonE identity coexist in the codebase without reconciliation. If θ = −lonE is the 'unified' coordinate, the 0.9941 scaling should be unnecessary. Its persistence suggests V13 was never fully implemented, or that the 'unified' coordinate requires post-hoc corrections to produce acceptable distances — which undermines the claim that it is 'unified.'

code_search.found_in: coordinates.txt: 'theta = delta_lon x 0.9941' (V12 formula). model.txt: 'EW angular scale: 0.9941' and 'theta = delta_lon x 0.9941'. ai-context.txt: 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' listed as open question (OPEN-003/V13 task 6). Note: gh CLI unavailable this session — could not search dome repo source code directly. Based on WIN-057's prior code search, distance_analysis.py applies 0.9941 as a constant with no derivation.
code_search.notes: θ = −lonE requires no derivation — it is the identity function on globe longitude. The 0.9941 factor has no derivation (confirmed open question on dome's own AI context page). Neither value is computed from dome physics. WIN-058 is listed in V51.0 wins (V50.6 had only 39 WINs going to WIN-039). No code or detailed exposition of WIN-058 found in dome source material — the claim appears to be a one-line assertion.

structural_pattern: WIN-058 is the dome's Rosetta Stone. It explicitly states that the dome's angular coordinate equals the globe's longitude — the most direct possible admission that the dome coordinate system is the globe coordinate system in disguise. Combined with the radial coordinate (WIN-057: latitude→r via WGS84), the full picture emerges: the dome's 'coordinate system' is WGS84 (lat, lon) → (r(lat), −lon) with r(lat) computed from a globe-calibrated exponential. Every dome distance calculation begins with WGS84 inputs and aims to reproduce WGS84 outputs. The dome is not an alternative cosmology; it is a lossy compression of the globe.
suggested_text: WIN-058's identity θ = −lon<sub>E</sub> is the defining property of an azimuthal equidistant (AE) projection centered on the North Pole: the angular coordinate IS geographic longitude. This is not a discovery or prediction — it is the dome admitting its coordinate system is the AE projection of the globe. Combined with the radial coordinate (WGS84 latitude → r via H(r)), both dome axes are globe coordinates in disguise. The dome has no native coordinate system independent of WGS84.
